$schema.description:
The URI of the JSON Schema that describes and validates this document.

You can point to a specific version of the schema, for example:
- `"https://pixi-vn.web.app/schemas/1.13.0/schema.json"` — pin to a specific version
- `"https://pixi-vn.web.app/schemas/latest/schema.json"` — always use the latest version

Most editors (VS Code, WebStorm, etc.) use this field to provide
auto-completion, hover documentation, and validation for the document.

initialOperations.description:
The operations to be executed before the narrative starts.
For the set storage: They will be set only if there are no variables with the same key already.
For the det tempstorage: if there are variables with the same key already, they will be overwritten.

labels.description: The labels to be used in the narrative. They will be added to the system

PixiVNJsonValueSet.description: Union of all "set value" operations — writes to storage, flag storage, or params.

PixiVNJsonOnlyStorageSet.description: Sets a value in either the persistent storage or the temporary (session) storage.

value.description: Value to be set in the storage

storageType.description: Type of the storage, if it is a flagStorage or a storage.

PixiVNJsonValueGet.description: Union of all "get value" operations — reads from storage, params, labels, choices, or a logic expression.

PixiVNJsonStorageGet.description: Retrieves a value from the persistent storage, flag storage, or temporary storage.

storageType.description:
Type of the storage, if it is a flagStorage or a storage.
If it is a flagStorage, the value will be get with the function

key.description: Index of the parameter in the params array.

PixiVNJsonLabelGet.description: Retrieves the number of times a label has been opened.

storageType.description: If it is a label, the value will be get with the function

PixiVNJsonChoiceGet.description: Retrieves the number of times a specific choice has been selected.

storageType.description: If it is a choice, the value will be get with the function

PixiVNJsonLogicGet.description: Retrieves a value from the logic/arithmetic layer (result of a computation or condition).

operation.description: The arithmetic or conditional operation whose result is the retrieved value.

leftValue.description: Left value of the arithmetic operation

operator.description:
Operator of the arithmetic operation:
- `"INT"` truncates the value to an integer (removes the decimal part)
- `"FLOOR"` rounds down to the nearest integer
- `"FLOAT"` converts the value to a floating-point number

PixiVNJsonConditionalStatements_StorageE.description:
A conditional statement that evaluates to a value of type `Then`.
It can be either:
- a  — selects a result from a list based on a strategy (random, sequential, loop)
- a  — evaluates a condition and returns the matching branch result,
  which may itself be another conditional or a

PixiVNJsonConditions.description:
Union of all condition types supported by PixiVNJson:
-  — binary comparison between two values
-  — truthy/falsy check on a plain value
-  — logical AND, OR, or NOT of other conditions
-  — arithmetic result used as a boolean (non-zero = true)

PixiVNJsonComparation.description:
Comparation for PixiVNJson.
In this comparation, the values to be converted to string and compared.

PixiVNJsonComparationOperatorsType.description:
Supported comparison operators.
- `"=="` equal
- `"!="` not equal
- `"<"` less than
- `"<="` less than or equal
- `">"` greater than
- `">="` greater than or equal
- `"CONTAINS"` checks whether the left string/array contains the right value

- `"or"` — at least one condition must be true

PixiVNJsonUnionConditionNot.description: Negates a single condition with a logical NOT.

PixiVNJsonFunction.description:
Invokes a named function and returns its result.
Functions are registered in the Pixi'VN runtime and can receive typed arguments.

functionName.description: The name of the registered function to call.

args.description:
Arguments to pass to the function. Each argument can be a condition, a raw value,
a value-get operation, or an arithmetic expression.

PixiVNJsonFlagSet.description:
Sets a boolean flag in the flag storage.
Flag storage is a dedicated boolean key-value store queried with .

value.description: Value to be set in the storage

storageType.description: Type of the storage, if it is a flagStorage or a storage.

PixiVNJsonOnlyParamSet.description: Sets a positional parameter value in the params temporary storage.

key.description: Index of the parameter in the params array to set.

value.description: Value to be set at the given param index.

condition.description: The list of conditions to be checked.

then.description: The value to be returned if the condition is true.

else.description: The value to be returned if the condition is false.

PixiVNJsonLabels.description: Collection of labels to be used in the narrative.

PixiVNJsonLabelStep.description:
Steps of a label.
Order of operations:
1. run all 
2. set , , 
3. open 
4. go to next step if  is true
5. end the label if  is "label_end"

operations.description: Operations to execute at the start of this step (storage writes, canvas updates, sound, etc.).

choices.description: Variable used to display a choice menu.

dialogue.description: Variable used to display a dialog.

glueEnabled.description:
This variable is used to add the next dialog text into the current dialog memory.
This value was added to introduce Ink Glue functionality https://github.com/inkle/ink/blob/master/Documentation/WritingWithInk.md#glue

labelToOpen.description: Variable used to open a label.

goNextStep.description: If is true, the next step will be executed automatically.

end.description:
Variable used to end some elements of the narrative.
- game_end: ends the game
- label_end: ends the label

conditionalStep.description:
If set, this step is replaced by the result of evaluating the given conditional statement.
Allows an entire step to be chosen at runtime based on a condition or step-switch strategy.

PixiVNJsonConditionalOperation.description:
A conditional operation — either a plain operation, an if-else that resolves to one,
or an operation-string (string interpolation expression).

PixiVNJsonOperation.description:
A single resolved operation — a value set, canvas operation, sound operation,
narration operation, or function call — optionally annotated with an origin string.

$origin.description: This value is used by the system to know where this operation is from, for example, if is generated from a string operation.

PixiVNJsonCanvas.description: Union of all canvas operations — show, edit, remove, animate, apply effects, and asset loading.

PixiVNJsonCanvasImageContainerShow.description: Shows an image container (a group of layered images) on the canvas.

alias.description: Unique identifier (alias) used to reference this element on the canvas.

urls.description: The ordered list of image URLs that compose the container layers.

props.description: Display options for the image container.

transition.description: Optional transition effect applied when the element appears on screen.

PixiVNJsonMediaTransiotions.description: Union of all supported canvas media transitions.

DissolveTransition.description: Cross-dissolve transition — blends the element in or out by gradually changing its opacity.

props.description: Configuration options for the dissolve transition.

priority.description: Pixi.js update priority for the transition ticker callback.

FadeTransition.description: Fade transition — fades the element in or out by animating its alpha value.

props.description: Configuration options for the fade transition.

priority.description: Pixi.js update priority for the transition ticker callback.

MoveInOutTransition.description: Move-in / Move-out transition — slides the element into or out of the viewport.

props.description: Configuration options for the move transition (direction, duration, easing, etc.).

priority.description: Pixi.js update priority for the transition ticker callback.

ZoomInOutTransition.description: Zoom-in / Zoom-out transition — scales the element into or out of view.

props.description: Configuration options for the zoom transition (scale, duration, easing, etc.).

priority.description: Pixi.js update priority for the transition ticker callback.

PushInOutTransition.description: Push-in / Push-out transition — pushes the element onto or off the viewport, displacing the current content.

props.description: Configuration options for the push transition (direction, duration, easing, etc.).

priority.description: Pixi.js update priority for the transition ticker callback.

PixiVNJsonCanvasImageVideoShow.description: Shows an image or video asset on the canvas.

alias.description: Unique identifier (alias) used to reference this element on the canvas.

url.description:
The url of the image or video.
If the url is not provided, the url will be set to the alias.

props.description: Display options (position, scale, alpha, etc.) for the image or video sprite.

transition.description: Optional transition effect applied when the element appears on screen.

PixiVNJsonCanvasTextShow.description: Shows a text element on the canvas.

alias.description: Unique identifier (alias) used to reference this element on the canvas.

props.description: Display options (font, style, position, etc.) for the text element.

transition.description: Optional transition effect applied when the element appears on screen.

PixiVNJsonCanvasEdit.description: Union of all canvas-element edit operations.

PixiVNJsonImageEdit.description: Edits the properties of an existing image sprite on the canvas.

alias.description: Alias of the canvas element to edit.

props.description: Partial memory/state properties to update on the image sprite.

PixiVNJsonVideoEdit.description: Edits the properties of an existing video sprite on the canvas.

alias.description: Alias of the canvas element to edit.

props.description: Partial memory/state properties to update on the video sprite.

PixiVNJsonImageContainerEdit.description: Edits the properties of an existing image container on the canvas.

alias.description: Alias of the canvas element to edit.

props.description: Partial memory/state properties to update on the image container.

PixiVNJsonTextEdit.description: Edits the properties of an existing text element on the canvas.

alias.description: Alias of the canvas element to edit.

props.description: Partial memory/state properties to update on the text element.

PixiVNJsonUnknownEdit_ImageSpriteMemory_.description:
Edits the properties of an unknown/generic canvas element.
Use this when the specific element type is not one of the built-in kinds.

alias.description: Alias of the canvas element to edit.

props.description: Partial options/properties to update on the canvas element.

PixiVNJsonCanvasRemove.description: Removes a canvas element from the stage, optionally with a transition.

alias.description: Alias of the canvas element to remove.

transition.description: Optional transition effect applied while the element disappears.

PixiVNJsonVideoPauseResume.description: Pauses or resumes playback of a video sprite on the canvas.

alias.description: Alias of the video element to pause or resume.

PixiVNJsonAssetsLoad.description: Loads or lazy-loads asset bundles or individual assets into memory.

aliases.description: List of asset aliases (or bundle names) to load.

PixiVNJsonCanvasAnimate.description: Union of all canvas animation operations — keyframe-based or sequence-based.

PixiVNJsonAnimateBase_T.description: Animates one or more canvas elements simultaneously using keyframe-based animation.

alias.description: Alias (or list of aliases) of the canvas elements to animate.

keyframes.description: The keyframes that define the animation values over time.

options.description: Additional options controlling the animation (duration, easing, repeat, etc.).

priority.description: Pixi.js update priority for this animation ticker callback.

PixiVNJsonAnimateSequence_T.description: Animates a canvas element using a sequence of segments (timeline-based animation).

alias.description: Alias of the canvas element to animate.

sequence.description: Ordered list of animation segments, each optionally including a transition.

options.description: Additional options controlling the sequence playback.

priority.description: Pixi.js update priority for this animation ticker callback.

PixiVNJsonCanvasEffect.description: Union of all canvas visual effects.

PixiVNJsonEffectShake.description: Applies a shake effect to a canvas element.

alias.description: Alias of the canvas element to apply the shake effect to.

props.description: Configuration properties for the shake effect (amplitude, duration, etc.).

priority.description: Pixi.js update priority for the effect ticker callback.

PixiVNJsonSound.description: Union of all sound operations — play, stop, pause/resume, and edit.

PixiVNJsonSoundPlay.description: Starts playback of a sound asset.

alias.description: Unique identifier (alias) used to reference this sound.

url.description: URL of the sound file. If omitted, the alias is used as the URL.

props.description: Playback options such as volume, loop, channel, and start offset.

PixiVNJsonSoundRemove.description: Stops a playing sound and removes it from the audio context.

alias.description: Alias of the sound to stop.

PixiVNJsonSoundPauseResume.description: Pauses or resumes a sound, a channel, or all audio.

alias.description: Alias of the sound or channel to pause/resume.

PixiVNJsonSoundEdit.description: Edits the properties of a currently playing sound (volume, speed, muted, loop, etc.).

alias.description: Alias of the sound to edit.

props.description: Partial set of properties to apply to the sound.

PixiVNJsonNarration.description: Narration operation — either a dialogue clear or a user-input request.

PixiVNJsonDialogue.description:
Clears (resets) the current dialogue state.
This is typically used to wipe any displayed text before showing new content.

PixiVNJsonInputRequest.description:
Requests user input during the narrative.
When this operation is encountered, the engine pauses and waits for the player to provide a value.

valueType.description:
The expected value type for the input (e.g. `"string"`, `"number"`, `"boolean"`).
Used by the engine to validate or cast the player's answer.

defaultValue.description: A default value that will be used if the player does not provide any input.

condition.description: The list of conditions to be checked.

then.description: The value to be returned if the condition is true.

else.description: The value to be returned if the condition is false.

PixiVNJsonOperationString.description:
An operation string that holds a list of string/value fragments to be concatenated at runtime.
Used internally to represent string interpolation expressions before they are fully resolved.

values.description: The ordered list of string fragments and/or value-get expressions to concatenate.

PixiVNJsonStepSwitchElementsType_PixiVNJ.description: The elements collection of a step-switch — either a plain array or a conditional that produces an array.

PixiVNJsonStepSwitchElementType_PixiVNJs.description:
A single element that can appear inside a step-switch, which may be a direct value,
a conditional statement, or a result-to-combine.

PixiVNJsonConditionalResultToCombine_Pix.description:
This element is used in case a  gives a result that must be combined with another calculated through other .
in case this possibility is not managed, it will be taken into consideration

combine.description:
Defines how the two results are combined:
- `"cross"` — cartesian product / pairwise combination of both results
- `"union"` — concatenation / merge of both results into a single collection

firstItem.description: The first (primary) result item, used as a fallback if the second conditional produces no result.

secondConditionalItem.description: The second conditional item(s) whose result will be combined with .

PixiVNJsonConditionalStatements_PixiVNJs.description:
A conditional statement that evaluates to a value of type `Then`.
It can be either:
- a  — selects a result from a list based on a strategy (random, sequential, loop)
- a  — evaluates a condition and returns the matching branch result,
  which may itself be another conditional or a

params.description:
**It is not recommended to use it, use it only if necessary**. The parameters to be passed to the label. If you want to pass an object, use the  attribute.
"params" attribute will be stored in the temp storage with the key:  + ( - 1).

PixiVNJsonAnimateBase.description: Animates one or more canvas elements simultaneously using keyframe-based animation.

alias.description: Alias (or list of aliases) of the canvas elements to animate.

keyframes.description: The keyframes that define the animation values over time.

options.description: Additional options controlling the animation (duration, easing, repeat, etc.).

priority.description: Pixi.js update priority for this animation ticker callback.

PixiVNJsonAnimateSequence.description: Animates a canvas element using a sequence of segments (timeline-based animation).

alias.description: Alias of the canvas element to animate.

sequence.description: Ordered list of animation segments, each optionally including a transition.

options.description: Additional options controlling the sequence playback.

priority.description: Pixi.js update priority for this animation ticker callback.

PixiVNJsonUnknownEdit.description:
Edits the properties of an unknown/generic canvas element.
Use this when the specific element type is not one of the built-in kinds.

alias.description: Alias of the canvas element to edit.

props.description: Partial options/properties to update on the canvas element.

PixiVNJsonDialog.description:
A dialogue line, optionally attributed to a character.
When a plain `Text` value is used, no character attribution is included.

character.description: The character id that will speak.

$schema.description:
The URI of the JSON Schema that describes and validates this document.

You can point to a specific version of the schema, for example:
- `"https://pixi-vn.web.app/schemas/1.13.0/schema.json"` — pin to a specific version
- `"https://pixi-vn.web.app/schemas/latest/schema.json"` — always use the latest version

Most editors (VS Code, WebStorm, etc.) use this field to provide
auto-completion, hover documentation, and validation for the document.

initialOperations.description:
The operations to be executed before the narrative starts.
For the set storage: They will be set only if there are no variables with the same key already.
For the det tempstorage: if there are variables with the same key already, they will be overwritten.

labels.description: The labels to be used in the narrative. They will be added to the system
